reasoning: A referendum on the independence of Republika Srpska has been a frequently threatened but consistently deferred political tool of Milorad Dodik for years. While he announced in June 2024 that an independence referendum would be held, but with "no specific date set", subsequent reporting indicates that he failed to formalize this process. Crucially, a May 2025 report reveals that Dodik has recently been convicted and banned from political participation. Although his allies threatened secession and "the implementation of a constitution of Republika Srpska" in the days leading up to his conviction, direct military warnings from NATO and EUFOR in February 2025 deterred immediate action. With Dodik removed from political leadership and strong international intervention suppressing separatist maneuvers, there is no credible evidence that the government of Republika Srpska has scheduled, or will legally be capable of scheduling, an official date for an independence referendum by July 1, 2025. Over the remaining two months, such an announcement is exceptionally unlikely, establishing a final probability effectively at the lower bound.
